title: Irish Potato Pie
description: A classic comfort food dish from Ireland, made with tender potatoes and a crumbly pastry crust. Perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: Peel and dice the potatoes, then boil them in a large pot of salted water until tender.
While the potatoes cook, sauté the onion and garlic in butter until softened.
Drain the potatoes and mash them with milk, butter, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roll out the pie crust and place it in a 9-inch pie dish.
Spoon the mashed potatoes into the pie crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Dot the top of the potatoes with butter and sprinkle with chopped herbs, if desired.
Roll out the remaining pie crust and use it to cover the pie, crimping the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the potatoes are heated through.
Let the pie rest for 10-15 minutes before serving.
